title: 2026 Cybersecurity Assessment: The Gap Between Awareness and Resilience
summary: The 2026 Bitdefender Cybersecurity Assessment highlights a significant challenge where organizations have high awareness of cyber risk but struggle to translate it into operational resilience. Based on a survey of 1,200 IT and cybersecurity professionals, the assessment reveals surprising contradictions in current security practices. This indicates a growing disparity between understanding threats and effectively mitigating them.

title: 2026 Cybersecurity Assessment: The Gap Between Awareness and Resilience

title: 2026 Cybersecurity Assessment: The Gap Between Awareness and Resilience
summary: The 2026 Bitdefender Cybersecurity Assessment highlights a significant challenge for organizations: despite increased awareness of cyber risk, translating this into operational resilience remains difficult. Based on a survey of 1,200 IT and cybersecurity professionals, the findings reveal surprising contradictions in current security practices.

title: 2026 Cybersecurity Assessment: The Gap Between Awareness and Resilience
summary: The article highlights a significant disconnect between organizations' high awareness of cyber risks and their struggle to translate this into effective operational resilience. The 2026 Bitdefender Cybersecurity Assessment, based on a survey of IT and cybersecurity professionals, reveals surprising contradictions. This suggests that despite increased understanding, practical implementation of robust defenses remains a challenge.

title: 2026 Cybersecurity Assessment: The Gap Between Awareness and Resilience
summary: The 2026 Bitdefender Cybersecurity Assessment reveals a significant gap between organizations' heightened awareness of cyber risk and their struggles to achieve operational resilience. Based on an independent survey of 1,200 IT and cybersecurity professionals, the assessment highlights surprising contradictions in current security postures.

title: Unpatched Argo CD Repo-Server Flaw Could Let Attackers Take Over Kubernetes Clusters
summary: An unpatched flaw in Argo CD's repo-server component allows an unauthenticated attacker to run code if they can reach its internal network port. This critical vulnerability, discovered by Synacktiv, can lead to a full takeover of Kubernetes clusters. There is currently no fix or CVE identifier for this issue.

title: SharePoint RCE CVE-2026-45659 Added to CISA KEV After Active Exploitation
summary: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has added CVE-2026-45659, a high-severity Remote Code Execution (RCE) vulnerability in Microsoft SharePoint Server, to its KEV catalog. This flaw, with a CVSS score of 8.8, stems from the deserialization of untrusted data and is actively being exploited, requiring immediate attention.

title: =SharePoint RCE CVE-2026-45659 Added to CISA KEV After Active Exploitation
summary: =CISA has added a high-severity Remote Code Execution (RCE) flaw in Microsoft SharePoint Server, tracked as CVE-2026-45659 (CVSS 8.8), to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog. The vulnerability, stemming from deserialization of untrusted data, is currently under active exploitation.

title: =New ChocoPoC RAT Targets Vulnerability Researchers via Fake PoC Exploit Repos
summary: =A new data-stealing trojan, ChocoPoC, is being distributed through fake Python Proof-of-Concept (PoC) exploit repositories on GitHub, specifically targeting vulnerability researchers. When executed, the malware compromises the victim's machine, stealing saved passwords, browser cookies, and files, and grants attackers a remote shell.

title: =New ChocoPoC RAT Targets Vulnerability Researchers via Fake PoC Exploit Repos
summary: =Attackers are deploying a new data-stealing trojan, named ChocoPoC, disguised as fake proof-of-concept (PoC) exploit code on GitHub. This malware specifically targets vulnerability researchers who download and run these fraudulent repositories. ChocoPoC exfiltrates sensitive data like passwords, browser cookies, and files, and grants attackers a shell on the compromised machine.

title: =Google Disrupts NetNut Residential Proxy Network Spanning 2 Million Home Devices
summary: =Google, in collaboration with the FBI and Lumen, has successfully degraded NetNut, a major residential proxy network also known as Popa. This network illicitly transformed millions of home devices into rented relays, allowing third parties to route their traffic through compromised systems. Google's Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG) significantly reduced the network's pool of usable devices, curbing its operational capacity.

title: =SkillCloak Lets Malicious AI Agent Skills Evade Static Scanners with Self-Extracting Packing
summary: =Researchers found a way to evade static scanners for malicious AI agent skills using simple changes. A new technique called SkillCloak allows malware to remain undetected.

title: =New TrojPix Attack Leaks Data From Air-Gapped Systems via Video Cable Emissions
summary: =Researchers discover a new technique called TrojPix that can leak data from air-gapped systems via video cable emissions. The attack requires malware to be already present on the target machine.

title: =Suspected China-Aligned Hackers Exploit Roundcube Flaws Against Universities
summary: =China-aligned hackers exploit Roundcube flaws in US and Canadian university webmail software. They siphon credentials using patched critical security flaws.

title: =15-Year-Old GhostLock Flaw Enables Root and Container Escape on Most Linux Distros
summary: =A 15-year-old Linux kernel flaw, GhostLock, allows any logged-in user to gain full root control of a machine. The vulnerability affects most mainstream Linux distributions.

title: =China-Linked UAT-7810 Expands ORB Network With New LONGLEASH Malware
summary: =A Chinese threat actor UAT-7810 is expanding its Operational Relay Box (ORB) network using new LONGLEASH malware. The actor is targeting internet-facing networking devices.

title: =Top AI Agents Built to Catch Malicious Code Can Be Tricked Into Running It
summary: =AI coding agents like Anthropic's Claude Code and OpenAI's Codex can be tricked into running malicious code. The attack, called 'Friendly Fire', works when the AI is running in autonomous mode.

title: =Dormant GitHub Accounts Help Attackers Blend In While Mapping Corporate Orgs
summary: =Attackers are using dormant GitHub accounts and automated scraping tools to enumerate corporate GitHub organizations and repositories. This helps attackers blend in and map corporate orgs.

title: =Attackers Exploit 'Ill Bloom' Vulnerability to Drain $3.1 Million From Cryptocurrency Wallets
summary: =Attackers exploited the 'Ill Bloom' vulnerability to drain $3.1 million from cryptocurrency wallets. The flaw is due to weak randomness in generating recovery phrases for some wallet software.
